name: Cipher Digital Inc.

profile.description: Cipher Digital (formerly Cipher Mining) is a digital infrastructure company that has pivoted from Bitcoin mining to high-performance computing data center development and operations, securing long-term leases with hyperscalers including AWS and Google totaling $9.3 billion in contracted revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Successfully pivoted from Bitcoin mining to HPC data center infrastructure, securing $9.3B in contracted revenue with tier-1 hyperscalers AWS and Google [1]. AI Deployment (1/2): Barber Lake facility (300 MW, 10-year Fluidstack/Google lease) and Black Pearl (300 MW, 15-year AWS lease) represent direct AI infrastructure deployment [1]. Market Share (2/2): Among the first crypto miners to establish credible HPC data center operations, with Morgan Stanley awarding an Overweight rating at $38 target [1]. Pipeline (1/2): 600 MW total contracted capacity with additional sites under development. R&D Intensity (1/1): Strategic transformation demonstrates innovation in business model.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Mission evolved from Bitcoin mining to 'powering the digital economy through critical infrastructure,' which is competent but generic. Community Impact (1/2): Data centers create local jobs and tax revenue in Texas and other locations. Employee Retention (0/2): Only 66 employees with significant CFO transition (Ed Farrell retiring, Greg Mumford taking over October 2025) [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Tyler Page's $17.35M compensation package is 95% stock-based, aligning interests with shareholders.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Cipher reported full-year 2025 Bitcoin mining revenue of $223.9M but a GAAP net loss of $822.2M driven by $450M mark-to-market loss on convertible notes and asset impairments [1][3]. D/E ratio of 331% reflects $3.73B in high-yield bond offerings to fund data center construction. Forward P/E of 25.9 assumes profitability from HPC leases commencing H2 2026. Liquidity is strong at $754M unrestricted. Liquidity floor: 28M avg volume x $14.01 = $392M daily turnover passes.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Cipher's pivot to HPC data center infrastructure makes AI an existential enabler rather than a threat — the entire business model is built on providing physical compute infrastructure for AI workloads [1][2]. Long-term take-or-pay leases with hyperscalers provide revenue certainty regardless of AI model evolution. AI Opportunity (1/1): Direct AI revenue exposure through $9.3B contracted HPC leases with Google/Fluidstack and AWS, with delivery commencing July 2026.

infinite_game.summary: Cipher demonstrates mixed infinite game qualities. The strategic pivot from Bitcoin mining to HPC infrastructure shows long-term thinking and willingness to evolve, but the transformation is so recent that durability of commitment remains unproven. The 10-15 year lease structures suggest long-term orientation.

just_cause.analysis: The mission to build critical digital infrastructure for the AI era is purposeful but lacks the inspirational quality of a true Just Cause. The pivot appears driven by economic opportunity (higher margins, stable revenue) rather than a transcendent purpose [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: The leadership team includes experienced operators from technology, fintech, and energy sectors [2]. However, with only 66 employees managing multi-billion-dollar construction projects, execution risk is high. The CFO transition in October 2025 was orderly.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Cipher competes with Core Scientific, Applied Digital, and traditional data center operators for hyperscaler contracts. CEO Tyler Page's acknowledgment that the Fluidstack/Google deal 'firmly established our credibility in the HPC space' shows awareness of positioning relative to rivals [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: The complete strategic transformation from Bitcoin mining to HPC data centers — including rebranding to Cipher Digital — represents textbook existential flexibility [1][2]. The company recognized the superior economics of HPC hosting and pivoted before competitors.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Raising $3.73B in high-yield bonds and committing to multi-year data center construction requires significant conviction. Tyler Page called Q3 2025 'truly transformative,' demonstrating confidence in the strategic direction [2].

investment_thesis: Cipher Digital has secured $9.3B in contracted HPC data center revenue through long-term leases with AWS and Google, with delivery commencing July 2026 and projected $669M average annualized NOI through 2036 [1]. The company holds $754M in liquidity, has fully funded its Barber Lake and Black Pearl facilities, and Morgan Stanley rates the stock Overweight at $38 — implying 170% upside from current levels. The pivot from mining to infrastructure hosting captures the secular AI demand wave.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on extreme leverage ($3.73B in bonds), execution risk on simultaneous multi-hundred-megawatt builds, and the gap between contracted future revenue and current $822M net losses [1]. Revenue from HPC leases won't begin until H2 2026, creating a multi-quarter dead zone. The D/E ratio of 331% leaves no margin for construction delays or cost overruns. If AI infrastructure spending slows, Cipher's take-or-pay contracts provide protection but the equity could be crushed by debt service obligations.
key_catalysts: AWS Black Pearl 300 MW delivery commencing July 2026 with rent starting August 2026 — first material HPC revenue recognition
Barber Lake 300 MW facility with Google/Fluidstack completing build-out in Q4 2026, doubling HPC capacity
Potential additional hyperscaler contracts beyond the current 600 MW could drive re-rating toward data center peer multiples
key_risks: Construction delays at Barber Lake or Black Pearl could defer revenue recognition and strain liquidity despite $754M cash position
D/E ratio of 331% with $3.73B in bonds creates significant interest expense burden — rising rates or credit downgrades could pressure equity value
Customer concentration risk: two hyperscaler relationships drive entire $9.3B contracted backlog, and loss of either would be catastrophic
sources: Cipher Mining Q4 and FY2025 Business Update and Rebrand Announcement, February 2026
